title: Goldfish Growth
description: Do goldfish grow, and what are their typical size limits?
narrative: The user wants factual information on whether goldfish increase in size over time, including typical adult lengths for common varieties, growth rates, and factors that influence growth (e.g., tank size, diet, genetics). Relevant documents should discuss the biology of goldfish growth, size benchmarks, and any conditions that affect maximum size.

title: Wi-Fi vs Bluetooth
description: What are the differences between Wi‑Fi and Bluetooth in terms of technology, performance, power usage, range, and typical applications?
narrative: The user seeks a clear comparative overview of Wi‑Fi and Bluetooth, covering key attributes such as data rate, latency, power consumption, coverage area, security, and common use cases (e.g., internet access vs. peripheral connectivity). Relevant documents should explain both technologies and directly contrast them, providing factual details, advantages, disadvantages, and possibly a side‑by‑side comparison table.

title: US WWI Entry
description: Why did the United States voluntarily enter World War I?
narrative: The user is seeking a historical explanation of the motivations behind the United States' decision to declare war on the Central Powers in 1917. Relevant documents should discuss the political, economic, and ideological factors that led to the U.S. entry, such as unrestricted German submarine warfare, the Zimmermann Telegram, American economic interests, and President Wilson's idealistic goals. Sources that merely mention other nations' involvement or unrelated World War I events are not relevant.

title: Declaratory Judgment Definition
description: What is a declaratory judgment and how is it defined in law?
narrative: The user wants a clear, authoritative definition of a declaratory judgment, including its purpose, legal effect, and typical contexts of use. Relevant content should explain the concept, cite statutes or case law, and differentiate it from other types of judgments.

title: Right Pelvic Pain
description: What are the possible causes of pain localized to the right side of the pelvis?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of medical conditions that can produce right-sided pelvic pain. Relevant information should cover a range of etiologies—including musculoskeletal, gastrointestinal, urinary, gynecological, vascular, and referred pain sources—and discuss how factors such as age, sex, pregnancy status, and accompanying symptoms influence the differential diagnosis. Documents that list, explain, or compare these causes, describe typical presentation, and note when urgent evaluation is needed are considered relevant.

title: Social Determinants
description: What are the social determinants of health?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of the factors that influence health outcomes beyond medical care, including economic, educational, environmental, social, and policy-related determinants, with definitions and examples for each.

title: Legionella Pneumonia
description: Does Legionella pneumophila cause pneumonia?
narrative: The user is seeking medical information confirming whether infection with Legionella pneumophila can lead to pneumonia (Legionnaires' disease). Relevant documents should describe Legionella pneumophila as a causative agent of pneumonia, explain its pathogenic mechanism, clinical presentation, epidemiology, and differentiate it from other bacterial causes of pneumonia. Sources that focus on unrelated organisms or non‑pulmonary manifestations are not relevant.

title: Jamaica Weather
description: What are the current weather conditions and short‑term forecast for Jamaica?
narrative: The user wants up‑to‑date meteorological information for Jamaica, covering temperature, humidity, precipitation, wind, and any weather alerts for today and the next few days. Relevant results should provide real‑time or recent weather reports, forecasts, or official meteorological service data for Jamaica (including major locations such as Kingston, Montego Bay, and Ocho Rios). Documents that only discuss historical climate averages or unrelated travel information are not relevant.

title: CP Dysarthria Types
description: What are the different types of dysarthria that can occur in individuals with cerebral palsy?
narrative: The user is seeking a detailed overview of the dysarthria subtypes that are specifically linked to cerebral palsy, including descriptions of each type (e.g., spastic, ataxic, flaccid, mixed), their clinical characteristics, underlying motor speech impairments, and how they are diagnosed or classified in the context of CP. Relevant documents should focus on the relationship between cerebral palsy and these dysarthria forms, providing clear definitions, prevalence information, and examples of speech manifestations.

title: Robert Gray Identity
description: Who is Robert Gray and what is he known for?
narrative: The user is looking for a concise biographical overview of the individual named Robert Gray, including his primary occupation, notable achievements, and any distinguishing details that separate him from other people with the same name. Relevant documents should clearly identify the person, provide key facts about his life or career, and explain why he is notable.

title: Sous Vide Foods
description: What types of food can be prepared using the sous‑vide cooking method?
narrative: The user is looking for information about the categories of foods that are suitable for sous‑vide cooking, including examples of proteins, vegetables, fruits, and other ingredients, as well as any considerations (e.g., texture, cooking time, safety) that affect suitability. Relevant documents should provide lists or descriptions of foods that can be cooked sous‑vide, recipes or guidelines for each type, and any tips on preparation or limitations.

title: Flea Life Cycle
description: What is the total duration and stage‑by‑stage time span of a flea's life cycle?
narrative: The user is looking for factual information on how long it takes for a flea to develop from egg through larva, pupa, and adult stages, including typical time ranges under normal conditions and any factors (temperature, host availability) that may alter the timeline. Relevant documents should detail the length of each developmental stage and the overall life cycle, while documents focused solely on flea control, extermination methods, or unrelated pest information are not relevant.

title: Contour Plowing Benefits
description: What environmental or agricultural problems can be reduced by using contour plowing?
narrative: The user seeks information on the specific negative effects—such as soil erosion, surface runoff, sediment loss, nutrient leaching, and related water-quality issues—that are mitigated when contour plowing is applied. Relevant documents should describe how contour plowing lowers these impacts, provide quantitative or qualitative evidence of reductions, and may compare its effectiveness to other tillage or land‑management practices.

title: Salvation Army Founding
description: When was the Salvation Army founded?
narrative: The user is looking for the historical founding date of the Salvation Army, including the year (and optionally the exact date) it was established, the original name of the organization, its founder(s), and the location of its inception. Relevant documents must contain factual information about the organization's origin—such as the year 1865 (as the Christian Mission) and the 1878 renaming to the Salvation Army—along with any supporting context about William Booth and the early development of the movement.

title: Active Margin Definition
description: What is an active margin in geological terms?
narrative: The user is seeking a clear, concise definition of an active margin, including its key characteristics (e.g., tectonic activity, earthquakes, volcanic arcs, subduction zones) and examples of where they occur. Relevant documents should explain the concept, contrast it with passive margins, and may discuss associated geological processes and hazards.

title: RN vs BSN
description: What are the primary differences between a Registered Nurse (RN) and a Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) qualification?
narrative: The user is seeking a comparative overview of the RN and BSN credentials, including distinctions in educational requirements, licensing, scope of practice, career advancement opportunities, typical work settings, and potential salary differences. Relevant documents should explicitly discuss how an RN (often obtained via an associate degree or diploma) differs from a BSN (a four‑year bachelor's degree), and should address any implications for professional responsibilities, eligibility for leadership or specialized roles, and marketability in the nursing field.

title: Medicare Ventilation Definition
description: What is the official Medicare definition of mechanical ventilation?
narrative: The user seeks the precise definition used by Medicare (CMS) for mechanical ventilation, including any coding (e.g., HCPCS/CPT), clinical criteria, and coverage guidelines. Relevant documents should contain the Medicare policy language, definitions, and requirements that determine when a service is considered mechanical ventilation for billing and reimbursement purposes.

title: Trapezoid Midsegment
description: How can I determine the length and construct the midsegment of a trapezoid?
narrative: The user is looking for a clear method to find the midsegment (also called the median) of a trapezoid, including the geometric definition, the formula relating it to the lengths of the two bases, and step‑by‑step instructions or constructions for drawing it. Relevant documents should explain the properties of the trapezoid midsegment, provide the length calculation (average of the bases), and possibly include diagrams, proofs, or practical examples of constructing the segment in geometry problems.

title: AML Analyst Role
description: Explain what an AML (Anti-Money Laundering) surveillance analyst does, including responsibilities, required skills, and typical qualifications.
narrative: The user wants a detailed overview of the AML surveillance analyst profession. Relevant material should define the role, outline its core duties (e.g., monitoring transactions, identifying suspicious activity, preparing SARs, supporting compliance), list essential knowledge areas (financial crime, regulatory frameworks, data analysis tools), and describe typical education, certifications, and experience needed. Sources that only discuss generic analyst positions or unrelated government analyst roles are not relevant.

title: Thai Daily Life
description: What does a typical day look like for ordinary people living in Thailand?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive picture of everyday routines, activities, work, family life, food, transportation, and leisure of average Thai residents. Relevant documents should describe typical daily schedules, common occupations, meals, social interactions, and cultural practices, noting differences between urban and rural settings or regional variations. Sources that focus solely on tourism attractions, historical overviews, or purely statistical demographics without describing lived experience are not relevant.

title: SIGMET Definition
description: What is the official definition of a SIGMET?
narrative: The user seeks an authoritative, concise definition of a SIGMET (Significant Meteorological Information), including its purpose, the weather phenomena it covers, and the issuing authorities. Relevant documents should provide the standard aviation‑meteorology definition as found in ICAO, FAA, or comparable regulatory sources.

title: Concrete Floor Cost
description: What are the typical costs associated with installing interior concrete flooring?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed pricing information for interior concrete flooring, including material costs, labor rates, and any additional expenses such as surface preparation, sealing, staining, or polishing. Relevant documents should provide per‑square‑foot or per‑square‑meter price ranges, factors that affect cost (e.g., location, thickness, finish type), and comparisons with alternative flooring options. Sources that only discuss design ideas, installation tips without cost data, or unrelated flooring types are not relevant.

title: Swiss Food Popularity
description: What is the most popular food in Switzerland?
narrative: The user wants to know which single food item is considered the most popular or most widely consumed in Switzerland, based on recent surveys, consumption statistics, or cultural prominence. Relevant documents should identify the top‑ranking food (e.g., fondue, raclette, rosti, chocolate, etc.), provide quantitative or authoritative evidence of its popularity, and may discuss its cultural significance. Sources such as national food consumption reports, reputable surveys, or expert culinary analyses are appropriate. Documents that merely describe Swiss cuisine generally, list multiple dishes without ranking, or focus on regional specialties without indicating overall popularity are not relevant.

title: Warm‑blooded Sharks
description: Which shark species are warm‑blooded and how do they physiologically maintain elevated body temperatures?
narrative: The user seeks detailed information on shark endothermy, including a list of shark species that exhibit warm‑blooded characteristics (e.g., great white, mako, salmon, basking, thresher) and an explanation of the biological mechanisms—such as regional endothermy, rete mirabile circulatory systems, and muscular heat production—that enable these sharks to retain heat. Relevant documents must discuss the physiology of shark thermoregulation, compare it to typical ectothermic sharks, and provide scientific citations or clear descriptions of the processes involved.

title: Durable Medical Equipment
description: What items are included in durable medical equipment and how is it defined?
narrative: The user wants a clear definition of durable medical equipment (DME) and a comprehensive list of the types of items that qualify as DME (e.g., wheelchairs, hospital beds, oxygen concentrators, prosthetics, etc.). Relevant documents should explain the criteria for an item to be considered DME (such as Medicare or regulatory definitions) and provide examples or categories of equipment that meet those criteria.

title: Exon Definition
description: What is the definition of an exon in biological terms?
narrative: The user is seeking a clear, concise definition of "exon" as used in molecular biology, including its role as a coding segment of a gene that remains in the mature mRNA after splicing. Relevant documents should explicitly define exons, describe their relationship to introns, and may mention their function in protein coding, typical length, and relevance to gene expression.

title: Visceral Definition
description: What is the definition of the word 'visceral'?
narrative: The user seeks a clear, concise explanation of the term 'visceral', including its meaning, usage, and any relevant examples or etymology. Relevant documents should provide dictionary-style definitions, discuss the word's connotations (e.g., relating to deep feelings or internal organs), and illustrate its use in context. Content focused solely on medical conditions or unrelated topics (e.g., specific visceral pain cases) is not relevant.

title: Tracheid Tissue
description: What plant tissue are tracheids a component of?
narrative: The user wants a concise factual answer indicating the plant tissue that includes tracheids, specifically that they are part of the xylem (and thus the vascular system). Relevant documents should explicitly state that tracheids belong to xylem and may describe their role in water transport within the vascular tissue.

title: RSA Key Definition
description: What is RSA and how are its encryption keys defined and generated?
narrative: The user wants a concise, technical explanation of the RSA cryptosystem that includes a clear definition of RSA, the purpose and structure of its public and private keys, the key‑generation process (including prime selection and modulus calculation), typical key sizes, and how the keys are used for encryption and digital signatures. Relevant material should focus on the algorithmic and mathematical fundamentals of RSA keys rather than unrelated organizations or reports.

title: CIS Founders
description: Who were the founders of the Commonwealth of Independent States?
narrative: The user is looking for information about the individuals or state leaders who created the Commonwealth of Independent States, specifically the signatories of the Belovezha Accords (e.g., Boris Yeltsin, Leonid Kravchuk, Stanislav Shushkevich) and the context of the organization’s formation in December 1991. Relevant results should describe these founders, their roles, and the circumstances surrounding the CIS’s establishment.

title: LVH Causes
description: What are the underlying causes and risk factors for left ventricular hypertrophy?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of the etiologies of left ventricular hypertrophy, including primary cardiac conditions (e.g., hypertension, aortic stenosis), secondary systemic factors (e.g., obesity, metabolic syndrome), genetic and congenital disorders, and physiological adaptations (e.g., athletic training). Relevant documents should discuss pathophysiology, prevalence, and clinical significance of each cause, and may come from medical textbooks, peer‑reviewed articles, or clinical guidelines.

title: LPS Laws Definition
description: What is the definition of LPS laws and what do they encompass?
narrative: The user is looking for an authoritative explanation of "LPS laws," including the meaning of the abbreviation, the legal context in which the term is used, the scope of the statutes or regulations that fall under it, and any key provisions or examples of its application. Relevant documents should clearly define LPS laws, describe their purpose and jurisdiction, and cite the specific statutes or regulatory frameworks involved. Content that merely mentions unrelated acronyms (e.g., LP‑gas) or does not provide a definition of the legal term is not relevant.

title: Three Percenters
description: What are the Three Percenters and what do they stand for?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of the Three Percenters militia movement, including its origin, ideology, membership, activities, notable incidents, and public perception. Relevant documents should describe the group's history, goals, structure, and any significant events associated with it.

title: Military Suicide Causes
description: What factors contribute to suicide among military personnel?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of the underlying causes and risk factors for suicide in the armed forces, including psychological (e.g., PTSD, depression), operational (e.g., combat exposure, deployment cycles), social (e.g., stigma, family stress), and systemic (e.g., access to firearms, lack of mental‑health resources) elements. Relevant documents should discuss research findings, statistics, case studies, or expert analyses that explain why military members are at heightened risk and what contributes to suicidal behavior.

title: Theraderm Use
description: Identify the purposes and applications of Theraderm products.
narrative: The user wants to know what Theraderm is used for, including its intended skin‑care benefits, target conditions, and typical application methods. Relevant documents should describe Theraderm product lines, their claimed uses (e.g., moisturizing, anti‑aging, skin‑repair, acne treatment), recommended usage instructions, and any clinical or consumer evidence supporting those uses.

title: Famvir Indication
description: What medical conditions is Famvir (famciclovir) prescribed to treat?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative information on the approved therapeutic uses of the antiviral drug Famvir, including the specific infections or diseases (e.g., herpes zoster, genital herpes, chickenpox) for which it is prescribed, typical dosing contexts, and any relevant clinical guidelines. Relevant documents should clearly state Famvir's indications, target viruses, and treatment purposes.

title: Anthropology Environment Definition
description: What is the definition of "environment" in anthropological theory?
narrative: The user seeks a scholarly explanation of how anthropologists define the term "environment," including its conceptual scope in cultural ecology, human‑environment interaction, and related subfields. Relevant sources should provide formal definitions, citations from anthropology textbooks or peer‑reviewed articles, and discuss the role of the concept within anthropological research.

title: Axon Terminal Definition
description: What is the precise definition and description of an axon terminal (synaptic knob)?
narrative: The user seeks a clear, authoritative definition of an axon terminal, also called a synaptic knob, including its anatomical features, role in neurotransmitter release, and relationship to the presynaptic neuron. Relevant documents should explicitly define the term, describe its structure (e.g., swelling at the end of an axon, presence of synaptic vesicles, presynaptic membrane), and explain its function in synaptic transmission. Sources that only mention other parts of the synapse without defining the axon terminal are not relevant.

title: CDG Airport Location
description: Is Charles de Gaulle Airport located within the main city of Paris?
narrative: The user seeks a clear answer about whether CDG (Charles de Gaulle) Airport is considered part of Paris proper. Relevant information should describe the airport's administrative jurisdiction, its geographic position relative to Paris city limits, distance from central Paris, and any common misconceptions about it being 'in Paris'. Documents that confirm CDG lies in the suburb of Roissy‑en‑France, outside the municipal boundaries of Paris, satisfy the need.

title: Monotonic Function Example
description: What are some concrete examples of monotonic functions?
narrative: The user is looking for clear, illustrative examples of functions that are monotonic (either non‑decreasing or non‑increasing) on their domain. Relevant information should include simple mathematical functions (e.g., linear, exponential, logarithmic, power functions) together with a brief explanation of why each is monotonic, any conditions on the domain, and possibly a short description or graph. Sources that merely mention monotonicity without providing concrete examples are not relevant.

title: Spruce Physical Description
description: What are the morphological characteristics and appearance of spruce trees?
narrative: The user wants a detailed physical description of spruce trees, covering aspects such as typical height, growth habit, bark texture and color, needle shape and arrangement, branch structure, cone form, and any distinguishing visual features. Relevant documents should provide concise yet comprehensive information on these attributes.

title: Hydrogen Liquefaction Temperature
description: At what temperature does hydrogen become a liquid?
narrative: The user wants to know the temperature threshold at which hydrogen exists as a liquid, preferably under standard atmospheric pressure. Relevant information includes the boiling point (~20.3 K at 1 atm), the triple point (~13.8 K), and the critical temperature (~33 K). Documents that provide these numeric values, explain hydrogen’s phase diagram, or discuss conditions for liquefying hydrogen are considered relevant.

title: McDouble vs Double
description: What are the differences between a McDouble and a Double Cheeseburger at McDonald's?
narrative: The user is looking for a clear comparison of the McDouble and the Double Cheeseburger. Relevant information should describe the ingredients (number of beef patties, cheese slices, sauces, buns), nutritional facts (calories, fat, protein, sodium), price, portion size, and any regional or promotional variations. Documents that provide side‑by‑side comparisons, official McDonald’s menu descriptions, or credible nutrition analyses that address these aspects are considered relevant.
